expectations.text: DashboardLayout이 folder 기반 index.tsx 패턴으로 정리된다
expectations.evidence: The output is a single flat file `DashboardLayout.tsx`, not a folder with `index.tsx`. The transcript confirms the refactoring was done in-place: 'Changes made to components/DashboardLayout.tsx'. No folder-based restructuring occurred.

The transcript states the output is `components/DashboardLayout.tsx` — the flat file was refactored in place and remains at its original path. The executor explicitly says: 'Output: components/DashboardLayout.tsx — refactored to match project conventions'.
A dedicated `Sidebar` function component exists (line 94) rendering an `<aside>` with `data-testid="dashboard-sidebar"`, containing logo, navigation, and footer sections with clear JSX comments.
A dedicated `Header` function component exists (line 167) rendering a `<header>` with `data-testid="dashboard-header"`, containing mobile logo, desktop title, and right slot.
A `<main>` element exists at line 278 with `data-testid="dashboard-main"` and `className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto ..."`, rendering `{children}`.
`DashboardLayoutProps` declares `children: ReactNode` (line 11). The component renders `{children}` inside `<main>` at line 282. The component is a pure layout shell wrapping child content.

Responsive Tailwind classes are used throughout: sidebar uses `hidden md:flex md:w-60 lg:w-64`, header uses `md:px-6`, main uses `md:px-6 md:pb-6 lg:px-8`, mobile nav uses `md:hidden`, header title uses `md:block`/`md:hidden` for mobile/desktop switching.

expectations.text: 사용처 import가 새 경로에 맞게 정리된다
expectations.evidence: The file was not moved to a new path — it stayed at `components/DashboardLayout.tsx`. The transcript does not mention updating any import statements. Since no folder restructuring occurred, there is no new path to update imports to.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Searched the entire 290-line output file. None of the forbidden patterns (useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation) appear anywhere in the code. The component is purely presentational.
No folder was created. The output is a single flat file `DashboardLayout.tsx`. There is no `dashboardLayout/` folder to evaluate.

The output file is at `components/DashboardLayout.tsx`, not under `components/dashboard/`. The transcript notes that `components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/` already exists with a split pattern, but the executor refactored the flat file in place rather than moving it into that directory.

claims.claim: The project convention uses folder-based index.tsx pattern (components/Button/index.tsx, etc.)
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 2 lists `components/Button/index.tsx`, `components/ProductCard/index.tsx` as existing patterns, and `components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/` with sub-components.

claims.claim: Constant name changed from NAV_LINKS (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE) to navItems (camelCase)
claims.evidence: Output line 27: `const navItems: NavItem[] = [...]` uses camelCase naming.

claims.claim: Prop name changed from headerTitle to title and headerRight to right
claims.evidence: The Header component uses `right` prop (line 167), but the DashboardLayoutProps interface still uses `headerRight` (line 13), not `right`. The rename was only partial — applied to the sub-component but not the public API.

claims.claim: pnpm exec tsc --noEmit produced no errors in DashboardLayout.tsx
claims.evidence: Cannot verify from available outputs — only the final file is provided, not the typecheck output.

claims.claim: All data-testid attributes preserved for E2E test compatibility
claims.evidence: Cannot verify without seeing the original file's data-testid attributes. The output has data-testid attributes present, but we cannot confirm they match the originals.

claims.claim: Sub-components reduced from 7 to 3 logical components
claims.evidence: Output contains exactly 3 sub-components: Sidebar (line 94), Header (line 167), MobileNav (line 231), matching the claim.

suggestions.assertion: 사용처 import가 새 경로에 맞게 정리된다
suggestions.reason: This assertion is only meaningful if the file was actually moved. When the executor refactors in-place, it becomes vacuously true or trivially false depending on interpretation. Consider making it conditional on the folder restructure, or checking for the existence of a barrel export (re-export) from the old path.

Five assertions (folder-structure, flat-file-removed, folder-camelCase, domain-placement, import-updated) all test the same underlying outcome: was the file moved into a folder under components/dashboard/? A single structural assertion plus one naming assertion would suffice. The current set creates redundant failure signals that inflate the fail count without adding information.

No assertion checks whether the refactored code actually follows the project's naming conventions (camelCase constants, semantic type names, section separators). These are the substantive convention-alignment changes the executor made, yet they go unmeasured.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions are heavily weighted toward file/folder placement (5 of 11) while neglecting code-level convention alignment, which is the core of the eval prompt. The executor did meaningful convention work (renaming, restructuring sub-components, adding section separators) that no assertion captures.
